description:
Advances: specled.verification

Deliverable:
One-shot CLI override for the verification command timeout, so an agent (or human) can test a bigger budget without editing .spec/config.yml. Motivated by the s65.3 incident: the verifier agent burned a full investigation cycle proving 'timeout, not test failures' — with this flag the hypothesis test is one command.

- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex: add command_timeout_ms: :integer to the OptionParser strict list; resolve as opts[:command_timeout_ms] || config.verification.command_timeout_ms (line ~58). Verifier already handles nil -> 120_000 default, so precedence CLI > config > default falls out of the existing || chain.
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.validate.ex: same change (strict list ~line 36, resolution ~line 52).
- Moduledocs: document the flag and the precedence order on both tasks.
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md: new must requirement (CLI flag overrides config value which overrides the 120s default) + scenario + add the requirement id to the existing tagged_tests verification covers list.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json via mix spec.check (never hand-edit).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: additions to test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs and test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_validate_test.exs — flag beats config value; absent flag falls back to config; absent both falls back to default. Tag covering tests with the new requirement id (test-tag enforcement runs at error).
- Behaviors to demonstrate: mix spec.check --command-timeout-ms 1 forces a timeout on a slow command; the same setup passes with a generous flag value while config is unset.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.validate.ex
- test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/mix/tasks/spec_validate_test.exs
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- lib/specled_ex/config*
- .spec/config.yml

Out of Scope (intent):
- No verifier behavior change — the verifier already accepts command_timeout_ms as an opt; this ticket only adds the CLI source for it.
- No config schema change — config.verification.command_timeout_ms parsing is untouched.
- Do not change finding codes or messages (that is the follow-up ticket).
- Conventions: to_timeout/1 not :timer helpers; @impl with explicit behaviour module; escape interpolation in doc examples.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.check green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Would fail if: (flag tier) flag ignored -> config 1ms kills the slow command -> findings != 0; (config tier) config ignored -> falls to 120s default -> sleep completes -> no verification_command_failed finding -> assert_raise misses; (default tier) 120s default wrong/too small -> slow command fails -> findings != 0.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex, lib/specled_ex/config*, .spec/config.yml all absent from the diff (verified via name-only). Verifier default @default_command_timeout_ms 120_000 (verifier.ex:13) unchanged. No finding-code/message changes.
- Project verification: pass. compile --warnings-as-errors=0; mix test 826 tests 0 failures; format --check-formatted=0; deps.unlock --check-unused=0; mix spec.check=0 (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0; 7 branch findings are info-level, non-blocking).
- Manual repro of --command-timeout-ms 1 vs generous value: pass. `mix spec.check --command-timeout-ms 1` -> status=fail errors=69, verification_command_failed on the real tagged_tests aggregation commands, exit 1. `mix spec.check --command-timeout-ms 300000` -> status=pass errors=0, exit 0. Behavioral override proven end-to-end.
- Source-claim trace: OptionParser strict list includes command_timeout_ms: :integer in both (spec.check.ex:47, spec.validate.ex:42). Resolution `opts[:command_timeout_ms] || config.verification.command_timeout_ms` in both (spec.check.ex:62, spec.validate.ex:56). Resolved value threaded to verifier via SpecLedEx.validate(..., command_timeout_ms: command_timeout_ms) (spec.check.ex:85, spec.validate.ex:73). Not merely computed -- actually passed through.
- Audit-expanded scope, if any: none.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- The config-tier tests (spec_check_test.exs:150, spec_validate_test.exs:184) emit a harmless "/bin/sh: specled_cmd_XXXX.sh: No such file or directory" line: the 1ms timeout kills/cleans the temp script before sh execs it. The outcome is still a verification_command_failed finding, which is the config-timeout path taking effect, so the precedence discriminator (finding present vs absent) holds. Tests do not assert the specific failure reason, only that a finding is produced.
- Untracked .codex-runs/ directory present in the worktree (implementer's run log); not committed, not in the diff, not a scope concern.
- state.json was rewritten by the 1ms experiment then restored to the committed version by the subsequent passing run; tree is clean (git diff HEAD -- .spec/state.json empty), confirming the committed state.json is up to date.

title: CLI override for verification timeout: --command-timeout-ms on spec.check and spec.validate
